leadingIndicators.name: AI Server Demand Proxy
leadingIndicators.desc: Surge in "Info/Comm Products" (HS Code 8471/8517) signals massive GPU assembly backlog.
leadingIndicators.methodology: Calculation: (Dec 2025 Export Value / Dec 2024 Export Value) - 1. Tracks dollar value of orders received by ODM giants (Foxconn, Quanta) for AI servers.

leadingIndicators.name: Raw Chip Output Signal
leadingIndicators.desc: Rising export value vs. flat volume confirms rising ASP (Super Cycle).
leadingIndicators.methodology: Calculation: (Dec 2025 "Memory" Export Value / Dec 2024 Value). A divergence where Value > Volume indicates increasing Average Selling Price (ASP).

schema.accelerators: AI accelerator specs (brand, product, capacity, bandwidth, price)

meta.dataSources: TrendForce, Morgan Stanley, CSET, TechInsights, IEDM, public filings

fuel.note: Fallback value - live pull via FRED if API key set

iran_war_approval.change: Net approval of Iran war handling

peace_deal.latest: Iran claims Hormuz re-closed June 19 over Israel-Lebanon strikes. Vance disputes closure.

expectations.description: Expectations for economy over next year

gas_price_impact.description: Average household paying ~$40/month more for gas

consumer_data.source: U of Michigan Survey of Consumers

helium.impact: Semiconductor manufacturing, MRI machines, aerospace, fiber optics
helium.notes: STILL CRITICAL: Airgas at 50% allocation, declared force majeure. Recovery will take YEARS not weeks. Qatar production offline. Russia/Algeria cannot fill gap. No substitutes exist for advanced chip manufacturing.

strait_of_hormuz.impact: 20% of global oil, 25% of LNG transits
strait_of_hormuz.notes: Iran says Hormuz closed again over Israel strikes in Lebanon, citing 'breach of promise.' Vance says 'no evidence' strait is closed. Peace deal status uncertain. ~550 vessels stranded.

container_shipping.impact: Container rates, delivery times, inventory costs
container_shipping.notes: Red Sea PARTIALLY reopening. Some carriers resuming Suez routes. But rates still 25-40% elevated, Cape diversions still adding 10-14 days. June seeing rate spike due to early peak season.

oil_supply.notes: Brent at $81.55, down from ~$104 peak. Dropped 5% on peace deal. War premium unwinding. If deal holds, could reach $75-80.

petrochemicals.notes: Still elevated but easing with oil prices. Feedstock costs declining. Full normalization depends on sustained Hormuz access.

ammonia.notes: Still elevated. Natural gas prices easing but Middle East exports not fully restored. Food inflation risk remains.

neon.notes: Less affected by Hormuz. US/EU alternative sources ramping but not at full capacity. Prices holding steady.

automotive_parts.notes: Helium shortage still constraining chip supply. Shipping improving but not normalized. Battery materials (lithium +100%) still critical.

deescalation.name: Peace Deal Holds
deescalation.description: June 19 signing holds. Hormuz open, sanctions relief phased in, nuclear talks continue. Markets normalize over 2-3 months.

baseline.name: Partial Resolution
baseline.description: Deal signed but implementation delayed. Nuclear issues unresolved. Reduced but ongoing uncertainty.

escalation.name: Deal Collapse
escalation.description: Hardliners derail deal. Hostilities resume. Hormuz re-closed. Return to conflict scenario.

severe.name: Major Escalation
severe.description: Deal rejected, expanded military engagement, full regional war. Least likely given current momentum.

losers.reason: War premium fading, peace reducing urgency
Oil prices falling on peace deal
